ancestral_harbinger.name: Ancestral Harbinger
description: ## Ancestral Harbinger
‹SOURCE Monster Summoner's Handbook/6›

While all bloodragers tap into their bloodlines to fuel the arcane energies they use in battle, some have the ability to call directly to the spirits of their ancestors to aid them, summoning spirits to fight for them or inspire their allies. Ancestral harbingers are particularly common among Shoanti bloodragers and among Bonuwat tribes living along the coast of western Garund.

::ab[Spirit Guardian (Sp)]{icon=power-magic l2="An ancestral harbinger can call upon her ancestors to aid her in combat, summoning a weapon of force that functions as per ‹spell/spiritual weapon›." l6="Her ancestral spirit can take the form of a savage creature, functioning as per ‹spell/summon nature's ally II› (this can't be used to summon multiple creatures from the *summon nature's ally I* list)." l12="Her ancestor can manifest physically as a ghostly figure, functioning as per ‹spell/spiritual ally›." l18="Her ancestral spirit can manifest either as a single powerful creature or as multiple lesser ancestors, functioning as per ‹spell/summon nature's ally VI›." special="The harbinger chooses which spell this ability functions as at the time of casting, and can use this ability while in a *bloodrage.*" useInc=bloodrager~4~2 useF="2~1~4" replace="Uncanny dodge~bloodline feats gained at 6th and 18th levels"}

::ab[Spirit Servants]{icon=learn-magic-boost order=ap s5="Summon monster I" s8="Summon monster II" s11="Summon monster III" s14="Summon monster IV" ability="An ancestral harbinger adds *summon monster* spells to her class spell list and gain them as spells known at the levels shown below. These spells can be used only to summon types of monsters with strong ties to the ancestral harbinger's family history, ranging from clan totems to monsters of the same type as those famously killed by her forebears. For each of these spells, the ancestral harbinger selects a number of monsters she can summon equal to her Charisma bonus (minimum 1) to represent these spirits. These monsters are selected from the options on the summon monster creature list or the expanded summoning list that are appropriate for the spell level." passive="The ancestral harbinger has a mystic tie to the spirit servants summoned with this ability. When the ancestral harbinger is in a *bloodrage,* all of her spirit servants gain a +2 morale bonus to AC and on attack rolls, damage rolls, and saving throws. The spirit servants also gain 2 ‹rule/temporary hit points› per Hit Die. The morale bonus and temporary hit points last until the *bloodrage* ends." imp7="At 7th level and every 2 bloodrager levels thereafter, the ancestral harbinger can swap one selected monster from one of these spells for a new monster from the same level's summoning lists." imp11="The morale bonus increases to +3 and the number of temporary hit points per Hit Die increases to 3." imp20="The morale bonus increases to +4 and the number of temporary hit points per Hit Die increases to 4." replace="Improved uncanny dodge~the bloodline feat gained at 12th level"}

blood_conduit.name: Blood Conduit
description: ## Blood Conduit
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/82›
Blood conduits learn to channel their arcane might directly through their flesh, without the need for mystical words or gestures.

::ab[Contact Specialist (Ex)]{l1="A blood conduit selects a bonus feat from the following: ‹feat/Improved Bull Rush›, ‹feat/Improved Grapple›, ‹feat/Improved Reposition›, ‹feat/Improved Trip›, and ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike›. He does not need to meet the prerequisites to take this feat. He also adds those feats to his list of bloodline feats." icon=power replace="Fast movement" alter="Bloodline feats"}

::ab[Spell Conduit (Su)]{l=5 icon=magic swift="When he succeeds at a ‹rule/combat maneuver<s› check to bull rush, grapple, pin, reposition, or trip an opponent, or makes an unarmed strike against an enemy, he can cast a touch spell on the creature that he affected with the combat maneuver, requiring no further touch attack roll. If this spell would usually require a successful touch attack, his successful combat maneuver check counts as this attack." special="The blood conduit must be wearing light or no armor." replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Reflexive Conduit (Ex)]{l=14 icon=magic immediate="While wearing light or no armor, when the blood conduit is subject to a combat maneuver check made to bull rush, grapple, pin, reposition, or trip him, he can target his attacker with a bloodrager spell that has a range of touch. If the spell would normally require a touch attack, a blood conduit can attempt a combat maneuver check for this attack instead." replace="Indomitable will"}

bloodline_familiars.name: Bloodline Familiars
description: ## Bloodline Familiars
‹SOURCE Familiar Folio/16›
::prereq{other="Must have one of these bloodlines: aberrant, abyssal, arcane, celestial, destined, draconic, elemental, fey, infernal, or undead."}

::ab[Bloodline Familiar]{flavor="Those with an inherent connection to magic often attract creatures who feel a similar instinctive pull toward magical forces." l=1 icon=power-down ability="The bloodrager gains a ‹sidekick/familiar› (as a wizard's ‹ability/familiar›), treating her class level as her wizard level for the purposes of this ability." order=an info="In addition, the character gains bonus spells from her bloodline one level later than she normally would." replace="The 1st-level bloodline power"}

::ab[Familiar Abilities]{head icon=power info="GMs may use the following bloodline familiar abilities as written, or employ them as guidelines for devising bloodline familiar abilities for bloodlines not listed below."}

::ab[Squeezer (Ex)]{next prereq="‹blrgbloodline/Aberrant› bloodline" icon=power ability="The familiar gains the ‹umr/compression› ability, allowing it to move through an area as small as one-quarter its space without ‹misc/squeezing› or one-eighth its space when squeezing."}
::ab[Grotesque Appendages (Ex)]{next prereq="‹blrgbloodline/Abyssal› bloodline" icon=boost ability="The damage dice of each of the familiar's natural attacks increases ‹misc/by one «die »step›."}

::ab[Spell Catalyst (Su)]{next prereq="‹blrgbloodline/Arcane› bloodline" icon=boost ability="Spells you cast that target your familiar are treated as having a caster level 2 levels higher than your actual caster level."}

::ab[Heavenly Touch (Su)]{next prereq="‹blrgbloodline/Celestial› bloodline" icon=aid useMod=Cha3 ability="Your familiar can grant ‹umr/fast healing› 1 to an allied creature it's touching. This effect lasts a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1) or until the familiar stops touching the creature, whichever comes first." imp10="The familiar grants fast healing 2 instead." imp20="The familiar grants fast healing 3 instead."}

::ab[Foretold Touch (Su)]{next prereq="‹blrgbloodline/Destined› bloodline" icon=boost ability="The familiar gains a +1 luck bonus on attack rolls to deliver touch spells, and the DC of touch spells delivered by the familiar increases by 1." imp10="The bonus becomes +2 and the DC is now raised by 2." imp20="The bonus becomes +3 and the DC is now raised by 3."}

::ab[Dragon's Flight (Ex)]{next prereq="‹blrgbloodline/Draconic› bloodline" icon=power useInc=caster~2 useF="1~1~2~~4" useNC=1 useUnit=minute useM ability="The familiar can sprout draconic wings, granting it a fly speed of 30 feet with average maneuverability." imp10="The familiar's fly speed increases to 60 feet with good maneuverability." imp20="The familiar's fly speed increases to 90 feet."}

::ab[Dualistic Energy (Su)]{next prereq="‹blrgbloodline/Elemental› bloodline" icon=magic ability="When your familiar delivers a touch spell that deals energy damage of a type other than your chosen energy type, your familiar can choose to alter the spell so that half of the energy damage dealt is of the spell's original type and the other half is of your chosen energy type."}

::ab[Amusing Familiar (Su)]{next prereq="‹blrgbloodline/Fey› bloodline" icon=power ability="The familiar can fascinate other creatures as the ‹performance/fascinate› bardic performance, treating your caster level as its bard level and using your Charisma modifier for the purpose of calculating the Will save DC. The familiar cannot perform any other actions while using this ability."}

::ab[Hellish Aura (Su)]{next prereq="‹blrgbloodline/Infernal› bloodline" icon=def ability="Animals don't willingly approach the familiar unless the animal's master succeeds at a DC 15 Handle Animal, Ride, or ‹ability/wild empathy› check." imp10="The DC becomes 20." imp20="The DC becomes 25." special="Animal companions, familiars and mounts are immune to this effect."}

::ab[Unliving Physiology (Su)]{next prereq="‹blrgbloodline/Undead› bloodline" icon=down ability="The familiar is alive, but is treated as undead for all effects that affect undead differently from living creatures, such as ‹main/cure spells› and ‹ability/channel«ed» energy›."}

bloodrider.name: Bloodrider
description: ## Bloodrider
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/82›

In the world's wild lands, a mount is an advantage in both everyday life and the dealing of death. In many barbarian tribes, the true stature of a warrior is determined by his skill and ferocity on horseback. Other tribes measure it in skill atop whatever terrible mounts their people employ. A number of bloodragers are not only skilled in the art of mounted combat, but have learned to channel their arcane energies directly into their mount.
::ab[Fast Rider (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="The bloodrider is adept at pushing his mount to its limit. The speed of any mount the bloodrager rides increases by 10 feet." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Feral Mount (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power ability="The bloodrider gains the service of a feral mount. This ability functions as the druid's ‹sidekick/animal companion›, using the bloodrager's level - 4 as his effective druid level. This companion must be one he is capable of riding and suitable as a mount. A Medium bloodrider can select a ‹companion/camel› or a ‹companion/horse›. A Small bloodrider can select a ‹companion/pony› or ‹companion/wolf›, but can also select a ‹companion/boar› or ‹companion/riding »dog› if he is at least 8th level." special="Whenever a bloodrider is *bloodraging,* this feral mount gains a +2 morale bonus to its Strength." replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Blood Bond (Su)]{l=9 icon=boost flavor="The bloodrider and his feral mount gain a closer bond that allows the bloodrider to augment his mount based on his bloodline." passive="While the bloodrider is *bloodraging* and on his feral mount, he grants the mount all the immunities and resistances he gains from bloodline powers. Furthermore, whenever the bloodrager is affected by a spell or spell-like ability with the range of personal while on the feral mount, the feral mount also gains the benefit of that spell." replace="The bloodline feat gained at 9th level"}

bloody_knuckled_rowdy.name: Bloody-Knuckled Rowdy
description: ## Bloody-Knuckled Rowdy
‹SOURCE Melee Tactics Toolbox/13›

Bloodragers are frequently rough-and-ready, untrained scrappers who learn to exploit any advantage to gain the upper hand in a fight. The bloody-knuckled rowdy focuses on tricks and maneuvers that are quick and effective, duplicating combat styles from professional brawlers, street thugs, and even trapped animals. Rather than focusing on a single style, most bloody-knuckled rowdies dabble in multiple techniques, mixing and matching their array of vicious maneuvers to suit the occasion.
::ab[Bloody Knuckles]{icon=down info="The bloody-knuckled rowdy does not gain damage reduction."}

::ab[Reduced Spells Known]{icon=down info="The bloody-knuckled rowdy focuses more on fighting tactics and less on his natural arcane powers. He receives one fewer spell known at each spell level." alter=Spellcasting}
::ab[Pugilist (Ex)]{icon=power l=1 ability="A bloody-knuckled rowdy gains ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Combat Style Student]{l=2 icon=power ability="A bloody-knuckled rowdy gains a bonus feat. This bonus feat must be a ‹main/style feat› or a feat with a style feat as a prerequisite. The bloody-knuckled rowdy treats his bloodrager levels as monk levels when qualifying for and calculating the effects of style feats and feats that have style feats as prerequisites." imp5="The bloody-knuckled rowdy gains the ‹feat/Combat Style Master› feat. He doesn't need to meet the prerequisites for this feat." replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge"}
::ab[Hand-to-Hand Training (Ex)]{l=3 icon=melee head ability="A bloody-knuckled rowdy deals unarmed damage as a ‹class/monk› of a level equal to his bloodrager level - 2."}

::ab[Unarmed Strike]{next flavor="Relevant text from the monk class ability." icon=melee l1="Your unarmed strike deals 1d4 (Small), 1d6 (Medium), or 1d8 (Large) damage." imp4="Your unarmed strike now deals 1d6 (Small), 1d8 (Medium), or 2d6 (Large) damage." imp8="Your unarmed strike now deals 1d8 (Small), 1d10 (Medium), or 2d8 (Large) damage." imp12="Your unarmed strike now deals 1d10 (Small), 2d6 (Medium), or 3d6 (Large) damage." imp16="Your unarmed strike now deals 2d6 (Small), 2d8 (Medium), or 3d8 (Large) damage."}

crossblooded_rager.name: Crossblooded Rager
description: ## Crossblooded Rager
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/83›

While most bloodragers manifest only one bloodline, there are some that, through some quirk of heredity or the conjunction of other powers, manifest two. This combination of two distinct bloodlines can create a versatile and powerful rager who stands out among the horde.

::ab[Two Bloodlines]{icon=power choice="A crossblooded rager selects two different bloodlines. He gains access to the feats, bonus spells, and some of the powers of both bloodlines he is descended from, but at the cost of reducing mental clarity (see Drawbacks below)." special="If the crossblooded rager already has a bloodline from another class, one of the chosen bloodlines must be that bloodline."}
::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=info info="A crossblooded rager combines the bonus feat lists from both of his bloodlines and can select his bloodline bonus feats from this combined list."}

::ab[Bonus Spells]{icon=info choice="A crossblooded rager can select his bonus spells from either of his bloodlines." order=ca ability="He can also learn a lower-level bonus spell he did not choose in place of the higher-level bonus spell he would normally gain. Lower-level bonus spells learned in this manner are always the spell level that they would be if the crossblooded rager had learned them with the appropriate bonus spell."}

*For example: A 7th-level ‹blrgbloodline/aberrant›/‹blrgbloodline/abyssal› crossblooded rager has the choice of learning ‹spell/enlarge person› or ‹spell/ray of enfeeblement› as his bloodline bonus spell. If he selected* ray of enfeeblement *as his 7th-level bonus spell, at 10th level he could use his new bonus spell to learn* enlarge person *instead of ‹spell/bull's strength› or ‹spell/see invisibility›, and he would add it to his list of 1st-level spells known (just as if he had learned it as his 7th-level bonus spell).*

::ab[Bloodline Powers]{icon=power choice="At 1st, 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th, and 20th levels, a crossblooded rager gains one of the two new bloodline powers available to him at that level. He can instead select a lower-level bloodline power he did not choose in place of one of these high-level powers."}

::ab[Drawbacks]{icon=down info="The furious mix of arcane power inside a crossblooded rager forces him to constantly expend mental effort to remain focused on his current situation and needs. This leaves him with less mental resolve available to deal with external threats." order=np passive="A crossblooded rager takes a -2 penalty to all Will saving throws at all times, and does not gain the +2 morale bonus on Will saving throws while *bloodraging* - though he does gain a +1 morale bonus Will saving throws when he gains the *greater bloodrage* class feature, which increases to a +2 morale bonus on saving throws upon gaining the *mighty bloodrage* class feature."}

enlightened_bloodrager.name: Enlightened Bloodrager
description: ## Enlightened Bloodrager
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/120;Arcane Anthology/16›

Troubled by overwhelming rage and mystic forces beyond their comprehension, bloodragers have been traveling to the ‹rule/Magaambya› for generations, seeking the peace and enlightenment promised by students of Old-Mage Jatembe. Many perish or succumb to their bloodlust long before arriving at the school, but those who survive that journey and dedicate themselves to Jatembe's teachings eventually learn to control their anger and suppress the worst influences of their bloodlines.

Once a bloodrager takes the teachings of the Magaambya into her heart and mind, she unlocks a unique method of casting her violent, rage-fueled spells. As a result, an enlightened bloodrager's magic looks, feels, and functions quite differently from the magic of other bloodragers. In many cases, when an enlightened bloodrager encounters a more traditional bloodrager, the meeting is laced with tension and suspicion. The enlightened bloodrager is, of course, well aware of the other bloodrager's tendency toward violence, and the other bloodrager is often unnerved by the calm and reasoned mannerisms of his enlightened counterpart. Many bloodragers even take offense at this demeanor, interpreting the enlightened bloodrager as a pawn of intellectuals or as having dishonored her ancestry or traditions by abandoning emotion and wrath. As one can well expect, such confrontations rarely result in lasting friendships!

::ab[Enlightened Spellcasting]{icon=power-magic flavor="An enlightened bloodrager's unusually sophisticated outlook on the relationship between magic and emotional outbursts allows her to cast an expanded array of spells." l4="She gains the ability to know and cast 0-level spells, which are cast like any other spells, but don't consume spell slots and can be used again. She gains a number of 0-level spells known equal to those of a ‹class/bard› whose level is 3 lower than her bloodrager level. She selects these 0-level spells from the ‹main/spells_>druid«'s list› of 0-level spells. She also gains one 1st-level druid spell as a bonus spell known." l7="She gains one 2nd-level druid spell as a bonus spell known." l10="She gains one 3rd-level druid spell as a bonus spell known." l13="She gains one 4th-level druid spell as a bonus spell known." special="The bloodrager treats any druid spells she takes with this ability as bloodrager spells for the purposes of other abilities, and adds them to her bloodrager class spell list." ability="At 8th level and every three levels thereafter, she can swap out these spells using the normal rules for swapping out a spell. If she chooses to swap out one of her bonus druid spells, she must replace it with a druid spell of the same level. Any swapped-out spells are removed from her bloodrager class spell list." alter=Spellcasting replace="1st-level bloodline power~bloodline bonus spells"}

::ab[Enlightened Bloodrage (Su)]{icon=power l=4 passive="While *bloodraging,* an enlightened bloodrager can still use Intelligence-, Dexterity-, and Charisma-based skills and can use abilities that require patience or concentration." alter=Bloodrage replace="The bloodline power gained at 4th level"}

::ab[Bloodline Feat]{l=6 icon=power order=an ability="An enlightened bloodrager adds ‹feat/Expanded Arcana›, ‹feat/Nameless One›, and all feats that list Nameless One as a prerequisite to the list of feats she can select as a bloodline feat." replace="Damage reduction the bloodrager gains at 7th, 13th, and 19th levels" info="The bloodrager gains DR 1/- at 10th level, and this increases to DR 2/- at 16th level."}

::ab[Bloodline Powers]{icon=power l8="An enlightened bloodrager gains the bloodline power normally granted at 1st level." l12="She gains the bloodline power normally granted at 4th level." l16="She gains the bloodline power normally granted at 8th level." l20="She gains the bloodline power normally gained at 12th level." special="She still uses her full bloodrager level when making level-based calculations in regard to these abilities." alter="The bloodrager's bloodline powers"}

greenrager.name: Greenrager
description: ## Greenrager
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/83›

Typically, nature finds its greatest harmony with divine magic, but sometimes a connection with the natural world manifest itself through the arcane current in the veins of the bloodragers called greenragers. These bloodragers funnel their eldritch heritage into abilities that allow them to call powerful allies from nature and empower them with their bloodrage.

::ab[Unfettered Fury (Ex)]{icon=power l3="A greenrager's fury allows him to move through undergrowth with frightening speed and grace. This functions as the druid's ‹ability/woodland stride› class feature, but only while the greenrager is *bloodraging.*" replace="Blood sanctuary"}

::ab[Summoning Rager (Sp)]{icon=learn l6="A greenrager's magic unlocks the secrets of summoning allies from nature. He adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally I› to his list of 1st-level bloodrager spells known as a bonus spell, as if it were a bonus bloodrager spell." l7="He adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally II› as a bonus 2nd-level bloodrager spell." l10="He adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally III› as a bonus 3rd level bloodrager spell." l13="He adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally IV› as a bonus 4th-level bloodrager spell." replace="The bloodline feat gained at 6th level"}

::ab[Furious Summoning (Su)]{icon=boost l9="Creatures summoned by the bloodrager's *summon nature's ally* spell gain a +4 morale bonus to Strength and Constitution and gain the druid's woodland stride ability." l11="This bonus increases to +6." l20="It increases to +8." replace="The bloodline feat at 9th level"}

hag_riven.name: Hag-Riven
description: ## Hag-Riven
‹SOURCE Blood of the Coven/27›

All ‹race/changeling«s› have the potential to transform into ‹family/hag«s›, but the process is a slow one, requiring several nights to complete. When left incomplete - by virtue of a wily changeling's escape or her mother's death - the creature is scarred and saturated in arcane power. Though she remains in possession of her own faculties, the seeds of hag hatred sprout within her. These hag-riven wield powerful magic interlaced with more powerful emotions.

The painful rituals that create a hag-riven can work for any creatures tenacious enough to endure them, and the art has sprung up as a defense against the devastating power of hags in various remote communities.
::prereq{other="Must select one of these bloodrager bloodlines: ‹blrgbloodline/arcane›, ‹blrgbloodline/destined›, ‹blrgbloodline/elemental›, ‹blrgbloodline/fey›, or ‹blrgbloodline/hag›."}
::ab[Arcane Influence]{icon=down passive="A hag-riven bloodrager does not gain proficiency with martial weapons." alter="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Claws of the Hag (Ex)]{icon=melee ability="A hag-riven grows a pair of vicious claws that are treated as ‹rule/natural weapon«s›. These claws are primary attacks and deal 1d4 points of damage each (1d3 for a Small creature) plus the hag-riven's Strength modifier." imp2="These claws are considered magic weapons for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction." imp5="The damage increases to 1d6 points (1d4 for a Small creature)." imp13="The hag-riven's claws threaten a critical hit on a natural roll of 19-20." imp16="The damage increases again to 1d8 points (1d6 for a Small creature)." replace="Fast movement~uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Sorcerous Claws (Su)]{icon=boost l=5 swift="A hag-riven can expend one of her daily spell slots to grant her claws an enhancement bonus equal to the level of the spell slot sacrificed. This bonus lasts for 1 minute and stacks with existing enhancement bonuses (such as those provided by ‹spell/magic fang› or magic items) to a maximum of +5." special="Multiple uses of this ability do not stack with themselves." imp7="The hag-riven can also expend some or all of the bonus provided by her sorcerous claws to add any of the following weapon properties to her claws for 1 minute: ‹magic-enh/flaming›, ‹magic-enh/flaming burst›, ‹magic-enh/frost›, ‹magic-enh/icy burst›, ‹magic-enh/keen›, ‹magic-enh/nullifying›, ‹magic-enh/seaborne›, ‹magic-enh/spellstealing›, ‹magic-enh/shock›, ‹magic-enh/shocking burst›, or ‹magic-enh/wounding›. Her claws must have a minimum enhancement bonus of +1 before any special abilities can be added." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Scarred Hide (Ex)]{icon=def l7="A hag-riven's scarred flesh becomes thick and leathery. She gains a +1 natural armor bonus to AC." l10="The bonus becomes +2." l13="The bonus becomes +3." l16="The bonus becomes +4." l19="The bonus becomes +5."}

::ab[Hexing Claws (Su)]{icon=power l10="A hag-riven gains ‹feat/Critical Focus› as a bonus feat, but she can apply its benefits only to her claw attacks. Each morning, she can select a single feat with Critical Focus as a prerequisite and apply its benefits to her claw attacks. She must meet all other prerequisites for any feat she wishes to apply with this ability." replace="Damage reduction"}

id_rager.name: Id Rager
description: ## Id Rager
‹SOURCE Occult Origins/23›
An id rager lacks a supernatural taint to his blood, instead drawing power from pure emotion.

:::ab{title="Atavistic Avatar (Su)" icon=power action=Choice imp1="When the id rager enters a *bloodrage,* he gains additional powers as if he were a ‹sidekick/phantom› with the emotional focus he selected as his atavistic focus. He is considered to be both a phantom and a ‹class/spiritualist› for the purposes of abilities whose effect references both a phantom and a spiritualist, such as a dedication phantom's *dutiful strike,* and treats his bloodrager level as both his spiritualist level and his phantom Hit Dice when determining abilities and save DCs. This ability does not allow the id rager to become incorporeal." imp4="The id rager gains ‹feat/Lightning Reflexes›, ‹feat/Great Fortitude›, or ‹feat/Iron Will› as a bonus feat." imp8="The id rager can meditate for 1 hour to change his atavistic focus for 24 hours or until he decides to return to his permanent atavistic focus as a @HLfree_action. When he changes his focus, he loses access to his Skill Focus feat and the feat he gained at 4th level, and he wields emotional focus powers as if his spiritualist level and phantom Hit Dice were 3 lower." imp12="The id rager gains Skill Focus in both of the skills associated with his atavistic focus. If circumstances cause him to lose one Skill Focus feat, he loses both." replace="Bloodline~all bloodline spells~all bloodline powers"}

An id rager chooses one emotional focus to define his core: anger, dedication, despair, fear, hatred, jealousy, kindness, remorse, whimsey, or zeal. He gains ‹feat/Skill Focus› as a bonus feat in one skill associated with his atavistic focus.
- **‹emotionalfocus/Anger›:** Intimidate or Survival
- **‹emotionalfocus/Dedication›:** Diplomacy or Sense Motive
- **‹emotionalfocus/Despair›:** Intimidate or Stealth
- **‹emotionalfocus/Fear›:** Intimidate or Stealth
- **‹emotionalfocus/Hatred›:** Acrobatics or Perception
- **‹emotionalfocus/Jealousy›:** Appraise or Bluff
- **‹emotionalfocus/Kindness›:** Diplomacy or Heal.@FN[1]
- **‹emotionalfocus/Remorse›:** Perception or Sense Motive
- **‹emotionalfocus/Whimsey›:** Acrobatics or Perception.@FN[2]
- **‹emotionalfocus/Zeal›:** Acrobatics or Survival

::ab[Atavistic Caster]{icon=magic l4="An id rager's bloodrager spells are treated as ‹rule/psychic magic›. The bloodrager's *bloodrage* does not prevent him from casting spells with emotional components, and he is considered to be a psychic spellcaster for the purposes of prerequisites (such as for the prerequisites of psychic duels and occult skill unlocks)." alter=Spellcasting replace="Eschew materials"}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power l6="An id rager can select one of the following feats as a bonus feat: ‹feat/Combat Casting›, ‹feat/Empath›, ‹feat/Extra Rage›, ‹feat/Intuitive Spell›, ‹feat/Logical Spell›, ‹feat/Psychic Combatant›, ‹feat/Psychic Defender›, ‹feat/Psychic Healing›, ‹feat/Psychic Maestro›, ‹feat/Psychic Virtuoso›, ‹feat/Raging Concentration›, ‹feat/Spell Focus›, or ‹feat/Spell Penetration›." l9="He can select a second bonus feat from the list." l12="He can select a third bonus feat from the list." l15="He can select a fourth bonus feat from the list." l18="He can select a fifth and final bonus feat from the list." replace="All bloodline feats"}
:::fakeFootnotes
@FN[‹source/Psychic Anthology› pg. 4]{from=1}
@FN[‹source/Heroes from the Fringe› pg. 17]{from=2}

metamagic_rager.name: Metamagic Rager
description: ## Metamagic Rager
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/83›
While metamagic is difficult for many bloodragers to utilize, a talented few are able to channel their bloodrage in ways that push their spells to impressive ends.

::ab[Meta-Rage (Su)]{icon=magic l=5 ability="A metamagic rager can sacrifice additional rounds of *bloodrage* to apply a ‹main/metamagic feat› he knows to a bloodrager spell. This costs a number of rounds of *bloodrage* equal to twice what the spell's adjusted level would normally be with the metamagic feat applied (minimum 2 rounds). The metamagic rager does not have to be *bloodraging* to use this ability. The metamagic effect is applied without increasing the level of the spell slot expended, though the casting time is increased as normal. The metamagic rager can apply only one metamagic feat he knows in this manner with each casting." special="Additionally, when the metamagic rager takes a bloodline feat, he can choose to take a metamagic feat instead." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

primalist.name: Primalist
description: ## Primalist
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/84›

While bloodrage powers come from the very essence of a bloodrager's being and are often strict and immutable, some bloodragers tap into ancient traditions and primitive wisdom to enhance their rages with something more primal. The primalist mixes his bloodline with more traditional rage powers.

::ab[Primal Choices]{icon=power l=4 choice="At 4th level and every four levels thereafter, a primalist can choose to take either his bloodline power or two ‹ability/barbarian »rage powers›. If the primalist chooses rage powers, those rage powers can be used in conjunction with his *bloodrage,* and his bloodrager level acts as his barbarian level when determining the effect of those bloodrage powers and any prerequisites. Any other prerequisites for a rage power must be met before a primalist can choose it." special="This ability does not count as the rage power class feature for determining feat prerequisites and other requirements." alter=Bloodline}

prowler_at_worlds_end.name: Prowler at World's End
description: ## Prowler at World's End
‹SOURCE Blood of the Beast/4›

Ancient catfolk legends claim their kind was created to protect the world from the sinister forces that exist at the world's fringes. Many ‹race/catfolk› bloodragers capable of communing with the ancient spirits of creation take on the burden of this ancient tradition.
::prereq{other="A prowler at world's end must choose the ‹blrgbloodline/destined› bloodline."}

::ab[Spirit]{icon=power-magic ability="A prowler at world's end gains the ability to channel the spirits of creation, granting him the ‹class/medium«'s› *spirit* and *spirit surge* abilities. A prowler channels the cheetah (which acts as the ‹spirit/trickster›), the leopard (which acts as the ‹spirit/guardian›), the lion (which acts as the ‹spirit/marshal›), and the tiger (which acts as the ‹spirit/champion›). The prowler does not have access to the archmage or hierophant legends. The favored locations for these spirits are altars, churches, sacred groves, and shrines, and their taboo requires the prowler to strongly revere nature and attempt to either destroy or incapacitate evil wherever he encounters it." imp1="A prowler at world's end gains his spirit's lesser power." imp8="He gains its intermediate power." imp16="He gains its greater power." special="A prowler's spirit surge bonus never advances beyond 1d6." replace="The 1st-, 8th-, and 16th-level bloodline powers" head}

:::ab{title="Spirit (Su)" icon=power-boost next flavor="Text from the medium class" special="After 24 hours, you lose contact with the channeled spirit and can perform another seance." action=Ability}

Each day, you can channel a ‹ability/spirit<s› through a ritual known as a seance. Seances take 1 hour to perform and require your concentration. At the end of a seance, you invite a local spirit to inhabit you and serve as your spirit for that day. You gain the spirit's listed seance boon and lesser spirit power for 24 hours.

Though you can choose the legend to which the spirit you channel belongs, you must channel that spirit from an appropriate location. Each spirit has a favored location it usually inhabits, though spirits may also be present at other locations appropriate to their legends; such locations are listed in each legend's **Favored Locations** entry.

You can invite your allies to participate in your seance: *shared seance* and certain spirit powers affect other participants. A character counts as participating so long as she maintains physical contact with another participating character and willfully opens herself to the spirit; unlike you, other participating characters can take other actions during this time. Only creatures with an Intelligence score of at least 3 can participate in a seance.

In addition to granting power to you, a channeled spirit can influence you. By channeling a spirit, you allow the spirit to gain 1 point of @HL[influence] over you. If you lose that 1 point of influence, you lose contact with the spirit, though you are still unable to perform a new seance until the normal 24 hour period has elapsed since your last seance. When the spirit leaves after the 24-hour duration and before the next seance, the spirit's influence over you resets to 0. Certain abilities allow you to gain additional power in exchange for allowing the spirit more influence over you.

When a spirit gains at least @HL[3 points of influence] over you, the dual impulses housed within the same body cause you to meld with your spirit's consciousness. The resulting muddled sense of self and struggle for control causes you to take a -2 penalty on initiative checks as well as a specific additional penalty listed in each spirit's entry. However, the spirit's mental presence grants you a +4 bonus against possession effects and a +2 bonus on saving throws against mind-affecting effects that are not related to possession.

If a spirit ever attains @HL[5 or more points of influence] over you, you completely lose control to the spirit. You effectively become an NPC under the GM's control until after you awaken the next day with the spirit gone.

During your seance, you can choose to @HL[channel a weaker spirit] to gain its help without incurring as much of its influence. If you do so, you can forgo any number of spirit powers, starting with your most powerful spirit power. For each spirit power you forgo, you can use your *spirit surge* ability twice without incurring influence.

::ab[Spirit Surge (Su)]{next flavor="Text from the medium class" icon=def passive="After failing a d20 roll that was modified by your spirit bonus, you can allow your spirit to gain 1 additional point of influence over you in order to add 1d6 to the check's result without taking an action. This can cause the check to succeed instead of fail. You must be conscious and aware to use this ability, and you can use this ability at most once per round."}

::ab[Bloodline Powers]{icon=power l4="A prowler at world's end gains the bloodline power normally granted by his bloodline at 1st level." l9="He gains the bloodline power normally granted by his bloodline at 4th level." l12="He gains the bloodline power normally granted by his bloodline at 8th level." l15="He gains the bloodline power normally granted by his bloodline at 12th level." l20="He gains the bloodline power normally granted by his bloodline at 16th level." alter="The 4th-, 16th-, and 20th-level bloodline powers" replace="9th-level and 15th-level bloodline feats"}

::ab[Chosen of the Spirits (Su)]{icon=magic l=11 ability="Whenever a prowler at world's end enters a *bloodrage,* he can apply the effects of ‹spell/beast shape IV› to himself instead of using *greater bloodrage* or *mighty bloodrage* to apply the effects of a bloodrager spell to himself. He must assume the form of a great cat of the same kind as a channeled spirit (cheetah, leopard, lion, or tiger)." special="This effect lasts for as long as the prowler continues *bloodraging,* regardless of its normal duration, and using it does not consume a spell slot." alter="Greater bloodrage~mighty bloodrage"}

::ab[Shapeshifted Spellcasting (Su)]{icon=magic l=12 ability="The prowler at world's end can cast spells while under the effects of *chosen of the spirits.* This ability functions as ‹feat/Natural Spell›, except the prowler uses the ability to cast while under the effects of *chosen of the spirits* instead of *wild shape.*" replace="The 12th-level bloodline feat"}

rageshaper.name: Rageshaper
description: ## Rageshaper
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/84›

All bloodragers blend the unpredictable surge of arcane power with the savage fury of battle lust. For most, their rage is a conduit for the eldritch power locked in their heritage, but for a rageshaper, the latent magical energies in his blood bring about physical transformations and facilitate the blending of arcana and aggression into a deadly synthesis that few other barbarians (or even other bloodragers) can match.

::ab[Bestial Aspect (Su)]{icon=magic-melee-boost l4="Whenever a rageshaper gains a ‹rule/natural attack› through the use of a polymorph spell, he can increase the damage done by that attack ‹misc/by one die›. If the spell grants multiple natural attacks, the rageshaper must choose one kind of natural attack for the ability to enhance." l9="If the rageshaper's altered form grants him a new mode of movement, that movement's base speed increases by 10 feet. This is an enhancement bonus." special="If the rageshaper's bloodrage powers already grant natural attacks or alternate modes of movement, then the bonuses granted by bestial aspect also apply to these bloodrage powers." replace="Blood sanctuary"}

::ab[Furious Transformation (Su)]{icon=magic-boost l=5 ability="A rageshaper can attempt to bolster any transmutation spell from the bloodrager spell list with the polymorph subschool while *bloodraging.* The rageshaper must succeed at a ‹rule/concentration› check as if casting defensively to modify the spell; otherwise, the spell is wasted. If he succeeds, the spell is treated as if the rageshaper had the ‹feat/Extend Spell› metamagic feat." special="The rageshaper must be the spell's intended target or cast a spell with a range of personal to gain this effect. If the rageshaper casts a spell that is linked to the rageshaper's own bloodline, such as a rageshaper with the ‹blrgbloodline/elemental› bloodline who casts ‹spell/elemental body I›, the spell is automatically extended without forcing the concentration check, so long as the rageshaper casts it while in a *bloodrage.*" replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

spelleater.name: Spelleater
description: ## Spelleater
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/85›

Where other bloodragers learn to avoid or shrug off minor damage of all sorts, spelleaters tap into the power of their bloodline in order to heal damage as it comes, and can even cannibalize their own magical energy to heal more damage and continue taking the fight to the enemy.

::ab[Blood of Life (Su)]{l=2 icon=aid flavor="A spelleater's blood empowers him to slowly recover from his wounds." passive="While *bloodraging,* a spelleater gains ‹umr/fast healing« 1›." imp7="This increases to fast healing 2." imp10="This increases to fast healing 3." imp13="This increases to fast healing 4." imp16="This increases to fast healing 5." imp19="This increases to fast healing 6." special="If the spelleater gains an increase to damage reduction from a bloodline, feat, or other ability, he is considered to have an effective damage reduction of 0, and the increase is added to this effective damage reduction." replace="Uncanny dodge~damage reduction"}

::ab[Spell Eating (Su)]{l=5 icon=aid flavor="A spelleater can consume spell slots for an extra dose of healing." swift="The spelleater can consume one unused bloodrager spell slot to heal 1d8 damage for each level of the spell slot consumed." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

steelblood.name: Steelblood
description: ## Steelblood
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/85›
Most bloodragers prefer light armor, but some learn the secret of using heavy armors. These steelbloods plod around the battlefield inspiring fear and delivering carnage from within a steel shell.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=def-magic ability="A steelblood gains proficiency in heavy armor. A steelblood can cast bloodrager spells while wearing heavy armor without incurring an arcane spell failure chance." replace="The bloodrager's armor proficiency"}

::ab[Indomitable Stance (Ex)]{icon=boost-def l=1 passive="A steelblood gains a +1 bonus on combat maneuver checks, to CMD against ‹rule/overrun› combat maneuvers, and on Reflex saving throws against ‹umr/trample› attacks. He also gains a +1 bonus to his AC against ‹rule/charge› attacks and on attack and damage rolls against charging creatures." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Armored Swiftness (Ex)]{icon=boost flavor="A steelblood moves faster in medium and heavy armor." l=2 passive="When wearing medium or heavy armor, a steelblood can move 5 feet faster than normal in that armor, to a maximum of his unencumbered speed." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Armor Training (Ex)]{icon=boost l=5 flavor="A steelblood learns to be more maneuverable while wearing armor." passive="Whenever he is wearing armor, he reduces the armor check penalty by 1 (to a maximum of 0) and increases the maximum Dexterity bonus allowed by his armor by 1." imp9="The penalty is now reduced by 2, and the maximum bonus is increased by 2." imp13="The penalty is now reduced by 3, and the maximum bonus is increased by 3." imp19="The penalty is now reduced by 4, and the maximum bonus is increased by 4." special="This ability stacks with the fighter class feature of the same name." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Blood Deflection (Su)]{icon=def l=7 immediate="A steelblood can sacrifice a bloodrager spell slot to gain a deflection bonus to AC equal to the level of the spell sacrificed. The deflection bonus lasts until the start of his next turn. This ability can be applied after an attack roll is made against the steelblood, allowing the steelblood to convert a hit into a miss if the deflection bonus is high enough." replace="Damage reduction"}

symbol_striker.name: Symbol Striker
description: ## Symbol Striker
‹SOURCE Heroes from the Fringe/7›

Symbol strikers are honored warriors of Dongun Hold, a Sky Citadel in Alkenstar that was a site of frequent conflicts between the nations of Geb and Nex. The dwarven inhabitants used powerful magic explosives to seal the fortress against the outside world and prevent the Sky Citadel from becoming a pawn in the arcane wars between the two powers. Only with the arrival of Ancil Alkenstar in 4588 AR did Dongun Hold reopen itself to the outside world. By then, the constant exposure to strange runic energies and the long isolation had caused a unique form of rune-wielding berserker spellcaster to develop, though the symbol striker tradition has since spread beyond its origins.
::cskill{gain=ling lose=ha}

::ab[Rune Training (Sp)]{icon=magic-learn ability="A symbol striker can cast ‹spell/read magic› at will and ‹spell/comprehend languages› once per day, but only to decipher written text. A symbol striker also adds ‹spell/arcane mark› and ‹spell/erase› to his bloodrager spell list as 1st-level spells, though he must select these as spells known to be able to cast them." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Weapon Rune (Su)]{l=6 icon=boost useInc=bloodrager~6 useF="6~1" standard="A symbol striker can imbue any spell he knows that has a range of touch or greater and a defined number of targets (rather than an area or other non-target-based effect) into a melee weapon he is holding. The spell forms itself into a brightly glowing rune, which is obvious to a casual observer. This ability functions only while the symbol striker wields the weapon. If dropped or taken, the weapon ceases granting this ability until it is returned to the symbol striker. The symbol striker can have only a single weapon rune active at a time; if he casts an additional weapon rune while a previous rune is active, the earlier rune fades. Weapon runes also fade when the symbol striker regains his spell slots for the day. Otherwise, the rune remains until the spell is discharged or dispelled." ability="Also as a @HLstandard_action, the symbol striker can make a melee attack with a weapon that has an active weapon rune and, if the attack is successful, deal normal weapon damage and also expend the weapon rune to discharge the stored spell into the target, as if delivering the spell with the ‹class/magus› spellstrike class feature." order=usa replace="Bloodline feats gained at 6th, 12th, and 18th levels"}

::ab[Rune Trap (Sp)]{l=9 icon=magic standard="The symbol striker can use a melee weapon to place a *rune trap* in any adjacent square. Any creature entering a square containing a *rune trap* takes 1d6 points of damage plus 1 additional point of damage for every 2 bloodrager levels the symbol striker has. This rune deals either acid, cold, electricity, or fire damage, which is decided at the rune's creation. The rune is clearly visible and lasts a number of minutes equal to the symbol striker's bloodrager level or until discharged." special="A symbol striker cannot create a *rune trap* in a square occupied by another creature. This rune counts as a 1st-level spell for the purpose of dispelling. A creature can disarm the rune by succeeding at a DC 26 Disable Device check." useMod=Cha3 replace="Bloodline feat gained at 9th level"}

untouchable_rager.name: Untouchable Rager
description: ## Untouchable Rager
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/85›

While most bloodragers are known for their inexplicable ability to focus their bloodline into a horrifying mix of martial terror and spellcasting fury, from time to time a bloodrager's bloodline acts differently. Instead of empowering the bloodrager, it shields the bloodrager from magic of all types, often keeping the bloodrager untouched within the midst of magical effects.

::ab[Raging Resistance (Ex)]{icon=def l=4 flavor="Instead of gaining spells, a bloodrager becomes resistant to spells." passive="While *bloodraging,* he gains spell resistance equal to 8 + his bloodrager level. Unlike normal spell resistance, the untouchable rager's spell resistance cannot be voluntarily lowered. As long as the untouchable is *bloodraging,* the spell resistance is persistent, and can only be lowered by ending the *bloodrage.*" special="At 7th, 10th, 13th, and 16th levels (when other bloodragers would gain bloodline spells), an untouchable rager's spell resistance from *raging resistance* is increased by 1." replace="Spells~blood casting~eschew materials~bloodline spells"}

::ab[Resistance Control (Ex)]{l=14 icon=def passive="An untouchable rager gains the spell resistance from *raging resistance* even while not in a *bloodrage.* Additionally, as long as the untouchable rager isn't *bloodraging,* he can turn off this spell resistance."}

urban_bloodrager.name: Urban Bloodrager
description: ## Urban Bloodrager
‹SOURCE Heroes of the Streets/24›

Like the ‹arc-barbarian/urban barbarian›, the urban bloodrager has learned to control her rage in so-called polite society. Though she lacks the untamed resilience of her wilder fellows, she's an expert at keeping her rage from causing collateral damage in crowds.
::cskill{gain=diplo~kl~ling~prof lose=ha~kna~sur}
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down info="An urban bloodrager isn't proficient with shields." alter="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Controlled Bloodrage]{icon=down-boost ability="When an urban bloodrager rages, she does not gain the normal benefits. Instead, she can apply a +4 morale bonus to her Constitution, Dexterity, or Strength. This bonus increases to +6 when she gains *greater bloodrage* and to +8 when she gains *mighty bloodrage.* She can apply the full bonus to one ability score or split the bonus between several scores in increments of 2." special="When using a *controlled bloodrage,* an urban bloodrager gains no bonus on Will saves, takes no penalties to AC, and can still use Charisma-, Dexterity-, and Intelligence-based skills. A *controlled bloodrage* still counts as a *bloodrage* for the purposes of any spells, feats, and other effects." alter=Bloodrage}

::ab[Restrained Magic (Su)]{l=3 flavor="An urban bloodrager can attune her spells so they are less likely to impact her allies or innocent bystanders." icon=protect ability="When the bloodrager casts a spell, she can grant a +2 bonus on the saving throw against that spell to any creatures she is aware of that are targeted by the spell or within the spell's area. Any creature that receives this bonus and succeeds at its save is unaffected by the spell, as if it hadn't been targeted or wasn't in the area." replace="Blood sanctuary"}

::ab[Adopted Magic]{icon=learn flavor="An urban bloodrager learns some of the secrets of other magical traditions from other denizens of the city." l7="She can select from the ‹main/spells_>bard› or ‹main/magus spell« list› any spell of a level she can cast, and add it to her bloodrager spell list and to her bloodrager spells known." l10="The urban bloodrager can add another such spell to her spell list and spells known." l13="She can choose and add another such spell." l16="She can choose and add another such spell." l19="She can choose and add another such spell." special="If she chooses to learn a new spell in place of an old one, she can exchange a bard or magus spell for another." replace="Damage reduction"}

@HL[Note:] The following bloodlines are especially suitable for an urban bloodrager, reflecting either a more nuanced origin or ancestry from city-dwelling magical creatures: ‹blrgbloodline/aberrant›, ‹blrgbloodline/arcane›, ‹blrgbloodline/celestial›, ‹blrgbloodline/destined›, ‹blrgbloodline/infernal›, and ‹blrgbloodline/undead›.
